body.title: Granny: config-driven worker dispatch rules in granny.yaml
body.result: dispatch_config.py with pure gate evaluator; granny.yaml schema with CC.0+DickSimnel.0; 28 tests; overnight time_window fixed
body.description:
Granny's worker dispatch logic is hardcoded Python. Move to config/profiles/granny.yaml: each worker entry declares dispatch_method, gates (time_window, usage_max_pct, max_concurrent, semaphore), and session (for tmux workers). Granny reads this at startup; run_once() evaluates gates generically — no per-worker if/else blocks.

Example schema:
  workers:
    CC.0:
      dispatch: tmux
      session: claude-main
      gates:
        time_window: '21:00-06:00'
        away_semaphore: CC.0.available.true
        usage_max_pct: 70
        max_concurrent: 1
    DickSimnel.0:
      dispatch: channel
      address: comms://dicksimnel
      gates:
        available_semaphore: DickSimnel.0.available.true
        max_concurrent: 1

**Affected files:** config/profiles/granny.yaml (new), devices/granny/daemon.py (config reader, gate evaluator), optionally devices/granny/dispatch_config.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; gate evaluation is a pure function (testable without I/O side effects); use PyYAML (already a project dep)
**Scope boundary:** IN: granny.yaml schema; config reader; generic gate evaluator replacing hardcoded checks; granny.queue.available.* semaphore support. OUT: actual CC.0 tmux dispatch (T-granny-cc0-dispatch), DickSimnel device (T-dicksimnel-device).
**Completion criteria:** Adding a worker entry to granny.yaml changes dispatch behavior without touching Python; gate evaluator tests cover time_window boundary (in/out), usage_max_pct threshold, semaphore absent/present.
